title: Fix SQL Query to Extract Unique Product Categories Correctly Sorted with Case Insensitivity

statement.markdown: A product catalog database contains a table named 'products' with columns 'product_id', 'name', and 'category'. A SQL query is needed to extract unique product categories from the table. The query should return the categories in alphabetical order, ignoring any case differences. The query also needs to handle empty strings as a special case, treating them as an empty string.
statement.inputFormat: The input will be a single table 'products' with columns 'product_id', 'name', and 'category'.
statement.outputFormat: The output will be a list of unique product categories in alphabetical order, ignoring any case differences.
statement.notes: The input table will contain at least one row.

constraints.description: Number of rows in the input table

examples.explanation: The query should return the unique product categories 'Electronics' and 'Toys' in alphabetical order.

hints.text: Use a SQL query with a GROUP BY clause to extract unique categories.
Use the UPPER() function to ignore case differences when sorting the categories.

editorial.title: Editorial: Fixing the SQL Query
editorial.intuition: The SQL query should use a GROUP BY clause to extract unique categories and the UPPER() function to ignore case differences when sorting the categories.
editorial.bruteForce: A brute force approach would be to use a SELECT DISTINCT statement to extract unique categories and then sort the result in a programming language.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution is to use a SQL query with a GROUP BY clause and the UPPER() function to extract unique categories in alphabetical order.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The proof of correctness involves showing that the SQL query returns the expected result for the given input table.
editorial.dryRun: A dry run of the SQL query with the input table shows that it returns the expected result.
commonMistakes: Not using the UPPER() function to ignore case differences
Not using a GROUP BY clause to extract unique categories

starterCode.javascript: // Define the SQL query function here

buggyStarterCode.javascript: var query = 'SELECT DISTINCT category FROM products ORDER BY LOWER(category)';
